symbols.name: ScopeEntry
symbols.docstring: Internal entry in the scope stack.

symbols.name: ScopeStack
symbols.docstring: Maintains the current scope chain during AST walking.

symbols.name: declare
symbols.docstring:
Declare a name in the current scope.

        Handles shadowing: first occurrence gets no suffix,
        second gets $2, third gets $3, etc.

        Returns the final symbol_id (potentially with $N suffix).

symbols.name: declare_in_scope
symbols.docstring: Declare a name in a specific scope (for global/nonlocal).

symbols.name: resolve
symbols.docstring:
Resolve a name by walking up the scope chain (LEGB).

        Skips class scopes — Python class scope is not accessible from
        nested function scopes via normal name lookup.

symbols.name: find_global_target
symbols.docstring: Find the module-level scope entry for `global` declarations.

symbols.name: find_nonlocal_target
symbols.docstring: Find the nearest enclosing function scope for `nonlocal` declarations.

symbols.name: find_enclosing_function_entry
symbols.docstring: Find the nearest enclosing function scope entry (for walrus in comprehensions).

symbols.name: find_enclosing_class
symbols.docstring: Find the nearest enclosing class scope, if any.

symbols.name: get_class_scope_entry
symbols.docstring: Get the ScopeEntry for a specific class scope ID.

symbols.name: build_scope_chain
symbols.docstring:
Build the dot-separated scope chain for symbol IDs.

        Module scope is represented by just the file_path.
        Named scopes (class, function) use dots.

symbols.name: build_symbol_id
symbols.docstring:
Build a symbol ID from the current scope chain.

        For scope-creating symbols (functions, classes), they use dot notation
        as they form part of the scope chain. For local names (variables,
        parameters), they use colon notation.

        Format: file:ScopeChain.With.Dots:local_with_colons
